records.ety: [OE. blak, AS. blæc; akin to Icel. blakkr dark, swarthy, Sw. bläck ink, Dan. blæk, OHG. blach, LG. & D. blaken to burn with a black smoke. Not akin to AS. blāc, E. bleak pallid. √98.]

defs: Destitute of light, or incapable of reflecting it; of the color of soot or coal; of the darkest or a very dark color, the opposite of white; characterized by such a color; as, black cloth; black hair or eyes.
quotes: O night, with hue so black!

defs: In a less literal sense: Enveloped or shrouded in darkness; very dark or gloomy; as, a black night; the heavens black with clouds.
quotes: I spy a black, suspicious, threatening cloud.

defs: Fig.: Dismal, gloomy, or forbidding, like darkness; destitute of moral light or goodness; atrociously wicked; cruel; mournful; calamitous; horrible.
Expressing menace, or discontent; threatening; sullen; foreboding; as, to regard one with black looks.
notes: ☞ Black is often used in self-explaining compound words; as, black-eyed, black-faced, black-haired, black-visaged.

defs: Sullenly; threateningly; maliciously; so as to produce blackness.
That which is destitute of light or whiteness; the darkest color, or rather a destitution of all color; as, a cloth has a good black.
quotes: Black is the badge of hell, The hue of dungeons, and the suit of night.

defs: A negro; a person whose skin is of a black color, or shaded with black; esp. a member or descendant of certain African races.
A black garment or dress; as, she wears black
Mourning garments of a black color; funereal drapery.
quotes: Friends weeping, and blacks, and obsequies, and the like show death terrible.
That was the full time they used to wear blacks for the death of their fathers.

defs: The part of a thing which is distinguished from the rest by being black.
quotes: The black or sight of the eye.

defs: A stain; a spot; a smooch.
quotes: Defiling her white lawn of chastity with ugly blacks of lust.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Blacked; p. pr. & vb. n. Blacking.]

defs: To make black; to blacken; to soil; to sully.
quotes: They have their teeth blacked, both men and women, for they say a dog hath his teeth white, therefore they will black theirs.

defs: To make black and shining, as boots or a stove, by applying blacking and then polishing with a brush.
